differential_diagnoses: Progressive multifocal leukoencephalopathy (PML) with cerebellar involvement
Leptomeningeal relapse of diffuse large B-cell lymphoma (DLBCL)
next_steps: Cerebrospinal fluid (CSF) polymerase chain reaction (PCR) testing for JC virus, VZV, HSV, HHV-6, and EBV
CSF cytology and flow cytometry repeat or specialized testing for malignancy
MRI of the spine to evaluate for leptomeningeal disease or other spinal processes
Consider brain biopsy of the affected cerebellar/brainstem region if infection is ruled out and clinical decline continues
Maintain high-dose acyclovir pending viral diagnostic results

differential_diagnoses: Leptomeningeal relapse of diffuse large B-cell lymphoma (DLBCL)
next_steps: Perform CSF PCR testing for JC virus, VZV, HSV-1/2, HHV-6, and EBV
Perform CSF immunoglobulin heavy chain (IgH) and T-cell receptor (TCR) gene rearrangement studies
Order immunohistochemistry for EBER (EBV-encoded RNA) and B-cell markers (CD20, CD79a) on cytospin preparations of CSF
Obtain MRI of the entire spine with and without contrast to look for leptomeningeal disease
Consult neurology/neurosurgery for assessment of surgical brain biopsy risks versus benefit
Submit serum and CSF for a comprehensive paraneoplastic and autoimmune encephalitis antibody panel
Maintain empiric high-dose intravenous acyclovir while awaiting viral PCR results
